channel.title: iLuvBnMe Blog & Podcast
channel.description: Tarot, astrology, human design, rituals, podcast transcripts, and self-love essays from iLuvBnMe.

item.title: A Love Letter to the Woman You're Growing Into
item.description: There is a version of you that already exists on the other side of this season. She is kinder with herself and surer of her yes. And here is the quiet secret: you don't have to reach her to be worthy of your own love right now. You already are her, just earlier in the story.

item.title: Full Moon in Aquarius: Releasing Who You Were
item.description: The full moon asks a hard question: who are you still carrying that you were never meant to keep? A reading and a ritual for letting go.

item.title: How to Read Your First Tarot Spread
item.description: The deck is not a test. This is the first thing I want you to know before you ever turn a card. You will not be graded on how many meanings you memorized, and the cards are not waiting to catch you getting it wrong. A tarot spread is a conversation, and you already speak the language.

item.title: The Empress & the Art of Radical Self-Care
item.description: The Empress knows that rest is not a reward. It is your birthright. On slowing down, receiving, and filling your own cup first.

item.title: Your Saturn Return, Made Simple
item.description: Somewhere around your twenty-ninth birthday, the ground may start to feel less like ground. Relationships you thought were solid reveal their cracks. The job that once fit begins to itch. You catch yourself asking, out of nowhere, "Wait, is this actually my life, or one I inherited?" If any of this sounds familiar, welcome. You are likely in your Saturn return.

item.title: Human Design 101: Trusting Your Strategy
item.description: An easy, loving intro to your energy type and how honoring it changes everything about how you move through the world.

item.title: The Moon Ritual I Return to Again and Again
item.description: I have tried the elaborate rituals. The ones with seven crystals arranged by the phase of the moon, the printed correspondences, the incense I could never quite identify. They were beautiful, and I never kept them up. The ritual that actually stayed with me for years is almost embarrassingly simple: a candle, a card, and a single question.

item.title: When the Cards Say Wait
item.description: Divine timing is real, and so is your impatience. An honest talk on trusting the pause and letting things unfold.

item.title: On Sovereignty: Choosing Yourself Out Loud
item.description: For most of my life I thought choosing myself was something you did quietly, apologetically, with a long explanation attached. I could rest, but only after I had earned it and reassured everyone that I still loved them. I could say no, but only if I softened it into a maybe first. Sovereignty taught me something different. Sovereignty says you are allowed to choose yourself out loud.

item.title: Venus Retrograde & Rewriting Your Worth
item.description: Venus turns inward and so do we, revisiting old stories about love, money, and what we believe we deserve.

item.title: Mercury Retrograde Without the Panic
item.description: Three or four times a year, the internet loses its mind. Do not sign the contract. Do not text your ex. Do not buy the laptop. Mercury is retrograde, and apparently we are all doomed. I want to offer you a calmer, truer story, because Mercury retrograde is not a punishment. It is an invitation, and once you learn to read it, you might even start to look forward to it.

item.title: You Are Allowed to Take Up Space
item.description: A short, fierce love letter for the woman who has spent too long shrinking. This is your permission slip.

item.title: The Star Card & Finding Hope After Hard Seasons
item.description: In the tarot, the Star always comes right after the Tower. That is not an accident, and if you know the Tower, you know exactly why it matters. The Tower is the card of sudden collapse, the lightning strike, the thing you thought was permanent crumbling in a single afternoon. And there, on the very next page of the deck, is the Star. The message is built into the order: after the worst of it, hope.

item.title: New Moon Intentions & the Star Card
item.description: Under a dark sky, we plant seeds. The Star card lights the way toward hope, healing, and quiet renewal.
